title: Investigate how to handle WSL2 enhancement available in the 'insiders'/store release; systemd support

comments.body: Since Windows 11 has a limited device install footprint (due to the TPM requirements), I think we should hold off on this, at least until it winds up in Windows 10
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
It looks like a Microsoft has this still in Preview. I think we are in a holding pattern before this is widely available.

It seems this now generally available, and interestingly also for Windows 10.
https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/the-windows-subsystem-for-linux-in-the-microsoft-store-is-now-generally-available-on-windows-10-and-11/
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@n1hility @gbraad what should we do with this issue?
We have to come up with a strategy on how to handle this. I have been using the systemd support and so far has been flawless

We have to decide to support older versions of WSL and keep using the nested namespace, and only support systend, or go for a detection (which eventually can be dropped) to run either setup when possible.
For what it's worth, this systemd mode is used by default for recent ubuntus https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/systemd , it's probably now mature enough to be used in podman-machine.
